digest.top_line: NRG-GU005: 5y DFS superiority not met (HR 1.38, futility crossed) for prostate SBRT vs MH-IMRT, but SBRT cut 2y bowel-QoL decline (35% vs 44%, p=0.034).
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day, all through an RT lens: GU005 says 5-fx SBRT isn't more effective than moderate hypofx but is less toxic (G≥3 GU 0.6% vs 2.5%, better bowel QoL), while Meier's 10-yr single-arm adds durability (OS 84%, RFS 90%) for 40Gy/5fx, no ADT. Head-neck: first ESTRO post-op oral-cavity CTV consensus.

sites.intro: First ESTRO consensus for post-op oral-cavity SCC target delineation, built to cut inter-institutional CTV variation and enable trial RTQA.

studies.name: ESTRO Oral Cavity CTV Delineation Guidelines
studies.tldr: New consensus recipe for post-op CTV: recreate pre-op GTV-P +10mm composited with surgical defect/flap +5mm; standardizes OCSCC delineation at EQD2 60 Gy.
details: 🔍 First ESTRO consensus for post-op primary + nodal CTV delineation in oral cavity SCC (OCSCC); no prior systematic guidance existed
🔍 Method: ESTRO multidisciplinary expert panel, then second-tier international review (Japan, HK, Australia, Brazil, others) for global applicability

subdetails: CTV-P = (GTV-P pre-op + 10mm) + (surgical defect/flap + 5mm), edited for air/bone/teeth
CTV-N1 = surrogate GTV-N + involved nodal level (post-op dose)
CTV-N2 = prophylactic at-risk nodal levels; plus uni- vs bilateral neck decision

details: 🔍 Surrogate GTV-P recreated on planning CT via MRI/CT registration or anatomical landmarks, since radical surgery leaves no residual GTV

details.text: 📐 Example dose: CTV-P/PTV EQD2 60 Gy (2 Gy fractions)

details: 🔗 Extends the 2018 primary-setting '5mm + 5mm' geometric-expansion CTV guidelines into the post-op setting
⚠️ Case examples: recreating GTV-P pre-op (not surgical defect alone) avoided geographical miss into the infratemporal fossa
⚠️ Expert consensus, not outcome-validated; goal is reduced inter-institutional variation + enabling RTQA in trials
studies.significance: The recipe is asymmetric: 10mm around the recreated pre-op GTV-P but only 5mm around the surgical defect/flap, composited. Recreating the GTV-P pre-op (not relying on the defect alone) is the load-bearing step; one buccal case would have missed the infratemporal fossa otherwise. Standardizes OCSCC post-op contouring at EQD2 60 Gy.
studies.monday_clinic: In resected oral cavity SCC referred for PORT, this defines a standardized post-op CTV recipe; it does not extend to definitive (non-operative) RT or to other head/neck subsites (larynx, oropharynx, hypopharynx), which await their own guidelines.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Consensus clinical guideline** from an ESTRO multidisciplinary expert panel; proposals drafted, then validated by a second tier of international HNSCC experts across many countries for worldwide applicability. Not a trial, **no outcome data**.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **Post-operative oral cavity SCC (OCSCC)** requiring PORT. Fills a gap: no prior systematic consensus for post-op primary + nodal CTV in mucosal HNSCC, a source of wide institutional variation.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **CTV-P post-op = (GTV-P pre-op + 10mm)** composited with **(surgical defect/flap + 5mm)**, edited for air/bone/teeth/fascial planes. Surrogate GTV-P/N recreated on the planning scan via MRI/CT registration or anatomical landmarks. Nodal **CTV-N1 = GTV-N + involved level**, CTV-N2 covers prophylactic at-risk levels, with uni- vs bilateral neck guidance. Example dose **EQD2 60 Gy (2 Gy/fx)**.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Extends the **2018** primary-setting CTV guidelines (**5mm + 5mm** geometric expansion) into the post-op setting. DAHANCA data showed geometric margins give **more conformal** CTVs than anatomical ones, and a protocol switch correlated with **lower late dysphagia** in an unplanned post-hoc analysis.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents resected oral cavity SCC receiving post-operative radiotherapy. Does not represent definitive (non-operative) RT or other head and neck subsites (larynx, oropharynx, hypopharynx, nasopharynx).

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Expert consensus, not outcome-validated.** The benefit is standardization (reduced inter-institutional variation, enabling RTQA and multi-institutional trials), not a demonstrated survival or toxicity endpoint.

open_questions: Whether standardized CTV margins reduce marginal recurrence vs current practice
Extension to other head/neck subsites beyond oral cavity

sites.intro: An all-radiation slate: NRG-GU005's randomized SBRT-vs-hypofx efficacy null (with a toxicity/QoL win), 10-yr single-arm SBRT durability, and ARS salvage-reirradiation criteria for radiorecurrence.

studies.name: 10-yr SBRT for Prostate Cancer (Meier Nonrandomized Trial)
studies.tldr: 10-yr OS 84%, RFS 90% (94% LR, 86% IR); late G3 GU/GI ≤1.5%, no G4-5; 40Gy/5fx SBRT, no ADT, 21 centers
details: 🔍 Phase 2 nonrandomized single-arm, N=310 evaluable across 21 community/regional/academic centers; median f/u 9 yr
🔍 172 low-risk (T1b-T2a, GS6, PSA≤10), 138 intermediate-risk (GS7 PSA≤10, or GS6 PSA 10-20); median age 68
📐 40 Gy/5fx (8Gy×5, ~100 Gy EQD2 at α/β=2) on noncoplanar robotic platform with real-time motion tracking
🔍 ADT not allowed; prostate volumes up to 100 cc eligible; treated 2008-2010
🔗 PACE-B (5-yr) showed SBRT noninferior to conventional EBRT for relapse + late toxicity; this adds 10-yr durability, single-arm
⚠️ Single-arm nonrandomized; no comparator arm (PACE-B carries the randomized evidence)
⚠️ RFS folds any salvage/antiandrogen/systemic therapy into relapse; biochemical failure scored nadir+2

verdict.rationale: Single-arm nonrandomized phase 2; no comparator arm (PACE-B provides randomized evidence). Mature 10-yr data reassure but design caps the read below confirmatory.
verdict.audience: Low- and intermediate-risk localized prostate, no ADT, Gleason ≤7, PSA ≤20

studies.significance: The unfavorable-IR subgroup is the read: 10-yr RFS 77% vs 92% favorable-IR, so SBRT monotherapy without ADT looks adequate for LR/favorable-IR but leaves unfavorable-IR pts short. 40Gy/5fx transfers directly to practice. Late GU G2+ 14% (vs GI 2.1%) is the toxicity to counsel.
studies.monday_clinic: In low- and favorable-intermediate-risk localized prostate (Gleason ≤7, no ADT indication), this supports ultrahypofractionated SBRT as durable at 10 yr; it does not extend to high-risk or node-positive disease, where ADT and nodal coverage remain in play.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Investigator-initiated **phase 2 nonrandomized** single-arm trial, N=**310** evaluable across **21** community, regional and academic centers, treated 2008-2010. Median follow-up **9 yr**; 10-yr Kaplan-Meier estimates.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: **172 low-risk** (T1b-T2a, Gleason 6, PSA ≤10) and **138 intermediate-risk** (Gleason 7 with PSA ≤10, or Gleason 6 with PSA 10-20), central path review. Median age 68; prostate volume up to 100 cc; **ADT not allowed**.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **40 Gy in 5 fractions** (8 Gy×5) on a noncoplanar robotic platform with real-time motion management, dose escalated to ~**100 Gy EQD2** (α/β=2).

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: 10-yr **OS 84%** and overall **RFS 90%**; risk-group and favorable/unfavorable IR breakdown in the results table. Relapse defined as biochemical failure (nadir+2), clinical failure, or salvage/systemic therapy.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Late **G3 GI/GU 1.4% LR, 1.5% IR**; **no grade 4-5**. G2+ GI 2.1% vs GU 14%, the dominant late burden. Physician-reported CTCAE v3.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: PACE-B showed SBRT noninferior to conventional EBRT at 5 yr; this extends single-arm SBRT durability to **10 yr**, consistent with the cohort's prior 5-yr low-toxicity report.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents low- and intermediate-risk organ-confined prostate cancer treated with SBRT without ADT. Does not represent high-risk, node-positive, or ADT-combined disease.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: **Single-arm**, no randomized comparator; toxicity is physician-reported (CTCAE v3), not patient-reported, so late GU burden may be understated; RFS folds salvage/systemic therapy into the relapse endpoint.

open_questions: SBRT monotherapy sufficiency for unfavorable intermediate-risk without ADT
10-yr randomized SBRT vs conventional EBRT outcomes

studies.name: American Radium Society AUC: Intraprostatic Recurrence After RT
studies.tldr: PSMA PET + mpMRI plus mandatory pre-salvage biopsy, then reirradiation (≤6 fx) over ADT alone, for radiorecurrent intraprostatic disease.
details: 🔍 ARS GU Appropriate Use Criteria; modified Delphi consensus across 4 topics: staging, biopsy, salvage modality, hormone therapy
🔍 Workup: PSMA PET + mpMRI to exclude metastatic disease and map local extent before salvage
🔍 Biopsy mandatory before local salvage; avoids treating radiographic change that is only treatment effect
📊 Salvage reirradiation preferred over noncurative ADT alone; most regimens ≤6 fractions
💊 Hormone therapy for radiosensitization only, short duration; classic ADT preferred over ARSIs
📊 Focal salvage when focal recurrence confirmed by imaging + tissue; whole-gland salvage toxicity called very tolerable

details.text: 🔗 RTOG 9408 post hoc (n=831): positive post-RT biopsy → higher BCR, HR 1.7, independent of upfront ADT

details: 🔗 Liu et al (n=79 radiorecurrent): PET vs conventional imaging concordant 56%; 34% upstaged, 10% downstaged
⚠️ Evidence base: conventionally fractionated EBRT only, workup pre-PSMA PET era; no randomized salvage trials
studies.significance: The RT-actionable read is workup discipline: mandatory biopsy before salvage (radiographic recurrence is often just treatment effect) and PSMA PET + mpMRI to exclude nodal/distant disease first. Preferred salvage is reirradiation in ≤6 fx over ADT alone, with short-course classic ADT (not ARSIs) for radiosensitization.
studies.monday_clinic: In a man with biopsy-confirmed isolated intraprostatic recurrence after definitive RT (mets excluded on PSMA PET/mpMRI), this supports curative-intent local salvage over ADT alone; it does not extend to nodal or distant failure.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **ARS Genitourinary Appropriate Use Criteria** consensus. PubMed + Embase across **four topics** (staging, biopsy, salvage modality, hormone therapy); evidence tables by 3 authors, panel voting via **modified Delphi** with RAND disagreement methodology.

analysis_sections.label: Discussion
analysis_sections.body: Workup: **PSMA PET + mpMRI** to exclude metastatic disease and map local extent; **biopsy mandatory** before salvage to avoid treating radiographic changes that are only treatment effect. **Salvage reirradiation preferred** over noncurative ADT alone, most regimens **≤6 fractions**; focal salvage when focal recurrence is confirmed, though whole-gland toxicity called very tolerable.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Evidence limited to pts initially treated with **conventionally fractionated EBRT** and worked up **before the PSMA PET era**; **no randomized salvage trials**, so recommendations rest on retrospective series and expert consensus.

open_questions: Optimal salvage reirradiation modality and dose/fractionation
Focal vs whole-gland salvage for intraprostatic radiorecurrence
Salvage outcomes in the PSMA PET era

studies.name: NRG-GU005
studies.tldr: DFS superiority not met (HR 1.38, futility crossed); SBRT improved 2y bowel QoL (35% vs 44% MCID decline, p=0.034) vs MH-IMRT.

details.text: 🔍 Phase III randomized non-blinded, N=698 (SBRT 353 / MH-IMRT 345), localized intermediate-risk prostate; co-primary DFS + EPIC-26 QoL

details: 📐 SBRT 36.25 Gy/5 fx (7.25 Gy/fx, 2-3×/wk) vs MH-IMRT 70 Gy/28 fx or 60 Gy/20 fx
🔍 CTV = prostate ±1 cm proximal SV; SBRT PTV 5 mm radial / 3 mm AP; fiducials + MRI fusion + daily IGRT mandated

details.text: 📊 Oncologic outcomes, SBRT vs MH-IMRT (superiority not met)
📊 EPIC-26 MCID decline by domain (fewer = better QoL), SBRT vs MH-IMRT
📊 Investigator-reported toxicity, any grade (all favor SBRT)

details: ⚠️ Efficacy superiority NOT met: DFS futility bound crossed; SBRT numerically favored IMRT on DFS and 3y biochemical control
🔗 vs PACE-B: both trials show lower urinary-incontinence QoL decline with SBRT vs MH-IMRT

details.text: Rectal spacer used in 56% SBRT / 55% IMRT; spacer → better EPIC bowel scores (LS mean -2.81, -4.49 to -1.13, p=0.0011)

verdict.rationale: Prespecified phase III; SBRT failed DFS superiority (futility crossed) and did not beat MH-IMRT on the urinary-irritation co-primary. Confirms SBRT as a safe equivalent option, not an upgrade.
verdict.audience: Localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer, definitive RT candidates

studies.significance: The urinary-irritation co-primary SBRT was powered for came back flat (35% vs 34%, p=0.68); the QoL edge is bowel and incontinence, not irritation. DFS superiority failed and 3y biochemical failure ran numerically higher on SBRT (8% vs 4%), so the case for 5-fx SBRT is convenience and lower GU/bowel toxicity, not better control.
studies.monday_clinic: In localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer weighing definitive RT fractionation, this supports 5-fraction SBRT as a shorter option with comparable disease control and a bowel and GU-toxicity edge over moderate hypofractionation; it does not establish SBRT as more effective.

primary_endpoint.name: Disease-free survival (co-primary)
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: 95% CI 0.91-2.09, p=0.13; DFS superiority not met (futility crossed)

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: **Phase 3**, randomized, non-blinded, international; **N=698** (SBRT 353 / MH-IMRT 345). **Co-primary**: disease-free survival (superiority) and EPIC-26 bowel + urinary-irritation QoL at 24 mo. Interim futility analyses prespecified.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Localized **intermediate-risk** prostate cancer, **median age 68**. Stratified by Gleason score, PSA, and rectal manipulation (SpaceOAR in ~55%).

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: **SBRT 36.25 Gy/5 fx** (7.25 Gy/fx, 2-3×/wk) vs **MH-IMRT 70 Gy/28 fx or 60 Gy/20 fx**. CTV = prostate ±1 cm proximal SV; SBRT PTV margin 5 mm radial / 3 mm AP. Fiducials, MRI fusion, daily IGRT mandated.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Co-primary DFS** powered for a 38% relative improvement (target HR 0.62). **Co-primary QoL**: absolute reduction in MCID for EPIC bowel (≥4) and urinary irritation/obstruction (≥5) at 24 mo.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **DFS superiority not met** (futility bound crossed); QoL split, bowel favored SBRT while urinary irritation did not. Effect sizes in the tables above.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Investigator-reported toxicity **favored SBRT**: fewer G≥3 GU events, less rectal hemorrhage, less fatigue (all p<0.05). Table above.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: Consistent with **PACE-B**: both show lower urinary-incontinence decline with SBRT vs MH-IMRT. MH-IMRT SOC anchored by **RTOG 0415**.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents localized intermediate-risk prostate cancer treated with definitive RT. Does not represent low-risk, high-risk, node-positive, or post-prostatectomy patients.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Non-blinded, so patient-reported QoL endpoints are unblinded. SBRT was designed to prove superiority and **failed** it; 3y biochemical failure ran numerically higher with SBRT.

open_questions: Durability of SBRT biochemical control beyond 3 years
Whether rectal spacer drives the bowel QoL benefit
